name: hass-client

metadata.name: hass-client
metadata.summary: Basic client for connecting to Home Assistant over websockets and REST.

documentation:
# hass-client
Very basic client for connecting to Home Assistant over websockets and REST.

modules.name: hass_client.const
documentation: Constants for the Home Assistant Websocket client.

modules.name: hass_client.utils
documentation: Various helpers and utilities.

functions.name: hass_client.utils.get_websocket_url
documentation: Extract Websocket URL from Home Assistant URL.

functions.name: hass_client.utils.is_supervisor
documentation: Return if we're running inside the HA Supervisor (e.g. HAOS).

functions.name: hass_client.utils.async_is_supervisor
documentation: Return if we're running inside the HA Supervisor (e.g. HAOS), async friendly.

functions.name: hass_client.utils.get_auth_url
documentation:
Return URL to auth flow.

The client ID you need to use is the website of your application.
The redirect url has to be of the same host and port as the client ID.

If client_id is omitted, it will be derived from the redirect url.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/auth_api#token

functions.name: hass_client.utils.get_token
documentation:
Return tokens given valid grants.

The token endpoint returns tokens given valid grants.
This grant is either an authorization code retrieved from the
authorize endpoint or a refresh token.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/auth_api#token

functions.name: hass_client.utils.get_long_lived_token
documentation: Request a Long Lived token with a short lived access token.

functions.name: hass_client.utils.base_url
documentation: Get the base URL for given url.

modules.name: hass_client.exceptions
documentation: Exceptions for hass-client.

documentation: Base Hass Client exception.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.TransportError

documentation: Initialize a transport error.
Exception raised to represent transport errors.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.CannotConnect

methods.name: hass_client.exceptions.CannotConnect.__init__
documentation: Initialize a cannot connect error.
Exception raised when failed to connect the client.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.ConnectionFailed

methods.name: hass_client.exceptions.ConnectionFailed.__init__
documentation: Initialize a connection failed error.
Exception raised when an established connection fails.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.ConnectionFailedDueToLargeMessage
documentation: Exception raised when an established connection fails due to an oversize message.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.NotFoundError
documentation: Exception that is raised when an entity can't be found.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.NotConnected
documentation: Exception raised when trying to handle unknown handler.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.InvalidState
documentation: Exception raised when data gets in invalid state.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.InvalidMessage
documentation: Exception raised when an invalid message is received.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed
documentation: Exception raised when authentication failed.

classes.name: hass_client.exceptions.FailedCommand
documentation: When a command has failed.

modules.name: hass_client
documentation: Provide a package for hass-client.

modules.name: hass_client.models
documentation: Models used for messages to/from the HA Websocket API.

classes.name: hass_client.models.AuthRequiredMessage

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.AuthRequiredMessage.type
documentation: auth_required

Message received in the auth phase (first message from server after connect).

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket#authentication-phase

classes.name: hass_client.models.AuthCommandMessage

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.AuthCommandMessage.type
documentation: auth

Message sent in the auth phase (first message from client to server).

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket#authentication-phase

classes.name: hass_client.models.AuthResultMessage

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.AuthResultMessage.type
documentation: auth_ok or auth_invalid

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.AuthResultMessage.message
documentation: in case of error

Message received as result on the auth command.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket#authentication-phase

classes.name: hass_client.models.Message
documentation:
Base Message format for exchanging messages with the server.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket/#command-phase

classes.name: hass_client.models.CommandMessage
documentation:
Message when sending a command from client to server.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket/#command-phase

classes.name: hass_client.models.Context
documentation: Context received in an event or result message.

classes.name: hass_client.models.CommandResultMessage
documentation:
Message when receiving the result of a command.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket/#command-phase

documentation: e.g. state_changed

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.Event.origin
documentation: e.g. LOCAL

Event object received in Event message.

https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/api/websocket/#subscribe-to-events

documentation: Representation of an (entity) State object as received from the api.

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.CompressedState.s

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.CompressedState.lc

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.CompressedState.lu

classes.name: hass_client.models.EntityStateEvent

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.EntityStateEvent.a
documentation: entity added (or first message)

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.EntityStateEvent.c
documentation: entity changed

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.EntityStateEvent.r

class_variables.name: hass_client.models.TokenDetails.token_type

modules.name: hass_client.client
documentation:
Home Assistant Client for python.

Simple wrapper for the Websocket API
provided by Home Assistant that allows for rapid development of apps
connected to Home Assistant.

variables.name: hass_client.client.MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE
documentation: 16MB

classes.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the connection to HomeAssistant.

Parameters:
- websocket_url: full url to the HomeAssistant websocket api or None for supervisor.
- token: a long lived token or None when using supervisor.
- aiohttp_session: optionally provide an existing aiohttp session.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.connected
documentation: Return if we're currently connected.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.version
documentation: Return version of connected Home Assistant instance.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.subscribe_events
documentation:
Subscribe to (all) HA events.

Parameters:
    - cb_func: callback function or coroutine
    - event_type: Optionally only listen for these event types (defaults to all.)

Returns: function to remove the listener.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.subscribe_entities
documentation:
Subscribe to state_changed events for specific entities only.

Parameters:
    - cb_func: callback function or coroutine
    - entity_ids: A list of entity_ids to watch.

Returns: function to remove the listener.

NOTE: The returned events are a compressed version of the state for performance reasons.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.call_service
documentation:
Call service on Home Assistant.

Parameters:
    - domain: Domain of the service to call (e.g. light, switch).
    - service: The service to call  (e.g. turn_on).
    - service_data: Optional dict with parameters (e.g. { brightness: 20 }).
    - target: Optional dict with target parameters (e.g. { device_id: "aabbccddeeffgg" }).

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_states
documentation: Get dump of the current states within Home Assistant.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_config
documentation: Get dump of the current config in Home Assistant.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_services
documentation: Get dump of the current services in Home Assistant.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_area_registry
documentation: Get Area Registry.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_device_registry
documentation: Get Device Registry.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_entity_registry
documentation: Get Entity Registry.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.get_entity_registry_entry
documentation: Get single entry from Entity Registry.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.send_command
documentation: Send a command to the HA websocket and return response.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.send_command_no_wait
documentation: Send a command to the HA websocket without awaiting the response.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.subscribe
documentation:
Instantiate a subscription for the given command.

Parameters:
    - cb_func: callback function or coroutine which will be called when a message comes in.
    - command: The command to issue to the server.
    - kwargs: Optionally provider any arguments.

Returns: function to remove the listener.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.connect
documentation: Connect to the websocket server.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.disconnect
documentation: Disconnect the client.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.start_listening
documentation: Connect (if needed) and start listening to incoming messages from the server.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient._handle_incoming_message
documentation: Handle incoming message.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient._send_json_message
documentation:
Send a message.

Raises NotConnected if client not connected.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.__aenter__
documentation: Connect to the websocket.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.__aexit__
documentation: Exit context manager.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient.__repr__
documentation: Return the representation.

methods.name: hass_client.client.HomeAssistantClient._get_message_id
documentation: Return a new message id.
Connection to HomeAssistant (over websockets).
